source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Unintentional Discharge of Drilling Fluid
actions_taken: Failed butterfly valve replaced with new valve
Additional gate valve installed at each transfer station
Low pressure test procedure implemented prior to fluid transfers
event.summary: On September 3, 2018, approximately 517 barrels of synthetic based mud (SBM) were inadvertently discharged into the Gulf of Mexico during a fluid transfer operation from the work boat 'Esplanade' to the H&P 100 platform rig. The discharge was caused by a gasket failure in a 4-inch butterfly valve combined with the absence of a secondary TODO Dry Break valve that had been removed but not reinstalled. The night crew failed to reinstall the TODO valve despite communication from the Deck Foreman that it needed to be in place prior to fluid transfer.
key_phrases: TODO Dry Break valve not installed
communication failure between day and night crew
lack of written requirement for TODO valve installation

hazards.name: Pressurized drilling fluid in transfer system
Synthetic based mud (SBM) with environmental hazard potential

threats.name: TODO Dry Break valve not installed or removed
Failure to reinstate secondary containment barrier

consequences.name: Unintentional discharge of 517 barrels of SBM to Gulf of Mexico
Environmental pollution - 245 barrels of pure base oil released

controls.name: 4-inch butterfly valve in transfer line
controls.barrier_role: Primary barrier to prevent fluid discharge
controls.lod_basis: Primary containment device in transfer system

supporting_text: The source of the leak was attributed to a gasket failure in the 4 inch butterfly valve
Each transfer line was equipped with a 4 inch butterfly valve and a TODO Dry Break valve

controls.name: TODO Dry Break valve (secondary spill prevention)
controls.barrier_role: Secondary barrier to prevent spill-free disconnect and fluid discharge
controls.lod_basis: Secondary containment mechanism in case primary barrier fails

supporting_text: The night rig crew did not re-attach the east side TODO valve which left the transfer hose open ended
The Deck Foreman stated that he communicated to the night crew that the east side TODO valve needed to be re-installed prior to the fluid transfer
During interviews, it was stated that the TODO valve was required to be installed as secondary spill prevention in case the butterfly valve failed

controls.name: Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA) review and sign-off
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure hazards are identified and mitigated
controls.lod_basis: Pre-job safety planning and crew awareness

supporting_text: Prior to commencing the transfer of the SBM drilling fluid, a Job Safety & Environmental Analysis (JSEA) was reviewed and signed by the Toolpusher, Driller, Assistant Driller, Derrickhand, and a Floorhand

There was not a requirement identified in any of the procedures, JSA's, or checklists that were available to the investigation team showing that the rig crew needed to have the TODO valve installed prior to fluid transfers

controls.name: Fluid transfer procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control defining safe transfer operations
controls.lod_basis: Procedural guidance for safe fluid transfer

supporting_text: Neither the Fluid transfer procedure or JSA identified the inspection of the transfer hose or TODO Dry Break valve
The Operator failed to follow their own transfer operation procedure, resulting in a non-permitted discharge

controls.name: Communication between day and night crew
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure critical information transfer

supporting_text: The Deck Foreman stated that he communicated to the night crew that the east side TODO valve needed to be re-installed prior to the fluid transfer
The night rig crew did not re-attach the east side TODO valve

controls.name: Manual intervention - All Stop procedure
controls.barrier_role: Emergency response to stop fluid transfer upon detection of problem
controls.lod_basis: Immediate response to stop ongoing discharge

supporting_text: At approximately 22:00 hours, he received a call on his hand held radio from the Motorman to shut-down the fluid transfer
The Floorhand initiated an 'All Stop' and immediately radioed the work boat to discontinue the transfer

controls.name: Replacement butterfly valve and additional gate valve installation
controls.barrier_role: Post-incident engineering improvement for redundant integrity
controls.lod_basis: Redundant containment barriers to prevent future discharges

supporting_text: Since the incident, H&P has replaced the failed butterfly valve with a new valve and installed an additional gate valve at each transfer station
The combination of the butterfly valves and gate valves at each transfer station gives redundant integrity to the mud system to prevent future discharges

controls.name: Low pressure test on mud system prior to fluid transfers
controls.barrier_role: Verification control to ensure system integrity before transfer

supporting_text: There will also be a low pressure test on the mud system prior to any fluid transfers to ensure the system is sealed as required prior to any transfer of fluids

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
